name: Sync contacts across systems with HubSpot and PostgreSQL on autopilot

parameters.query: SELECT * FROM jobs WHERE updated_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day' ORDER BY updated_at DESC;

INSERT INTO job_log (jobId, type, payload, logged_at)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3, NOW());

parameters.content:
### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Pull Side A (HubSpot)

### Pull Side A (HubSpot)

hubspot — `search` operation against the connected account.

Next: Diff the Two Systems

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Pull Side B (PostgreSQL)

postgres — `executeQuery` operation against the connected account.

Next: Diff the Two Systems

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Diff the Two Systems

Diffs two streams by key into 4 lanes: only-in-A, same, different, only-in-B — the heart of the sync.

Ports: ①→ Create Missing in PostgreSQL · ②→ Count In-Sync Rows · ③→ Apply Updates to PostgreSQL · ④→ Backfill into HubSpot

### Create Missing in PostgreSQL

postgres — `executeQuery` operation against the connected account.

Next: Gather Sync Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Count In-Sync Rows

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Gather Sync Results

### Apply Updates to PostgreSQL

postgres — `executeQuery` operation against the connected account.

Next: Gather Sync Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Backfill into HubSpot

hubspot — `contact` operation against the connected account.

Next: Gather Sync Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Gather Sync Results

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

# Sync contacts across systems with HubSpot and PostgreSQL on autopilot

**Pattern:** Two-System Sync · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Sync contacts across systems with HubSpot and PostgreSQL on autopilot. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual integrations is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Pull Side A (HubSpot) → Diff the Two Systems → Create Missing in PostgreSQL → Gather Sync Results

## Use it for
- Teams that want to two-system sync without manual effort
- Anyone automating integrations in productivity
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Responds in seconds
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: HubSpot private-app token; Postgres connection.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your integrations process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
